Off Calendar Oct-04-2022 Granted Per Written Order

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/RBU)

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Mar-09-2022 Set For Trial On Mar-20-23 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION FOR LEAVE TO Amend Answer (Motions)
Off Calendar Apr-15-2022 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT JAMES GATTS' Motion for Leave to Amend Answer.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant Gatts' motion to amend is granted.

Courts employ extreme liberality in allowing amendments to an answer. (See Hulsey v. Koehler (1990) 218 Cal.App.3d 1150, 1159 ["In particular, liberality should be displayed in allowing amendments to answers, for a defendant denied leave to amend is permanently deprived of a defense."].)

The court agrees that the proposed defenses are conclusory. (See FPI Development, Inc. v. Nakashima (1991) 231 Cal.App.3d 367, 384 [answer must plead ultimate facts just like a complaint]). But instead of denying amendment, the better practice is to allow amendment and subsequently test the pleading by motion/demurrer. (See Calif. Cas. Gen. Ins. Co. vs. Sup. Ct. (1985) 173 Cal.App.3d 274, 281 (overruled on other grounds in Kransco v. American Empire Surplus Lines Ins. Co. (2000) 23 Cal.4th 390.) The court would expect the parties to meet and confer and resolve the conclusory nature of the proposed defenses prior to any subsequent motion practice. (See, e.g., CCP 435.5; 430.41 [meet and confer requirement prior to demurrer or motion to strike].)

Plaintiff's other arguments to deny amendment have no merit. If the parties entered into a binding settlement agreement, that is a viable defense. Gatts is not attempting to plead an offer to compromise as grounds to hold plaintiff liable. There is also no basis to condition amendment on Gatts accepting liability, which is inconsistent with the defenses that he is attempting to plead. There is no prejudice to plaintiff given the current trial date in March of 2023.

Order signed.

Judge: Suzanne R. Bolanos; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported (302/SRB)
